source.description: Six-panel generated raster process sheet showing one comic firecracker doodle from construction to finished marker drawing.
source.notes: Subject, pose, and final elements were locked before image generation for the July 3, 2026 current daily lesson.

process_strategy: The firecracker body, fuse, bands, sparks, marker color, and shadow appear before the final pass, which only thickens and cleans existing work.

final_elements.name: tilted firecracker body
final_elements.description: A short diagonal cylinder with rounded ends.

final_elements.name: top cap and wrapper bands
final_elements.description: Cap and band lines wrapping around the firecracker body.

final_elements.name: curved fuse
final_elements.description: A bent fuse coming from one end of the firecracker.

final_elements.name: spark bursts
final_elements.description: Small star and dot bursts around the fuse tip.

final_elements.name: red and yellow marker fills
final_elements.description: Bright marker color on the existing body bands, cap, and sparks.

final_elements.name: gray sticker shadow
final_elements.description: A small offset shadow under the already drawn firecracker.

frames.visible_job: Draws the diagonal cylinder body guide with rounded end caps.
Adds cap and wrapper bands around the existing body.
Adds a curved fuse and thickens the main outside marker line.
Adds star bursts and dot sparks near the already placed fuse tip.
Adds red and yellow marker fills, white shine gaps, marker streaks, and the existing-shape sticker shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Thickens existing outlines and sharpens the already present bands, fuse, sparks, fills, highlights, and shadow.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
